$schema: This is descriptive research evidence, not normative doctrine
note: Cinematic movements and periods defined by color characteristics. All examples are illustrative only.

defining_color_characteristics: Vibrantly colored through tinting and toning techniques
technological_basis: Toning - replacing silver particles with colored salts
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Established foundational semiotics of film color before realistic color film invention. Color as enhancement of fantastical and spectacular elements.
key_films: A Trip to the Moon (1902) - famously hand-colored
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Created basic color-meaning associations (blue = night) that persist in cinema

defining_color_characteristics: Deep blacks due to silver key image
Colors designed and coordinated rather than captured
technological_basis: Beam-splitting camera recording separate red, green, blue records
Required massive lighting setups due to slow film speed
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Expensive process reserved for spectacle genres: musicals, historical epics, animation. Solidified Hollywood look of glamour and high visibility.
key_films: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Defined Golden Age Hollywood aesthetic, established Technicolor as premium experience

defining_color_characteristics: Democratized color for gritty, on-location productions
Unstable dyes leading to magenta shift over time
Color moved from spectacle to realism
New Hollywood earth tones and grain
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Decline of studio system, rise of location shooting and realism. Color fading crisis prompted archival activism and some return to black-and-white.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Democratized color filmmaking but created major archival preservation challenges

defining_color_characteristics: Color used to break continuity and realism
Color drawing attention to film as object
Plastic use of color like paint on canvas
technological_basis: Emphasis on color in costume and set design over lighting
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Anti-realistic aesthetics, modernist art influence, rejection of classical Hollywood narrative conventions.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Established color as formal element separate from realistic representation

defining_color_characteristics: Heavily gelled lighting in reds, greens, purples
Color as psychological and horror element
technological_basis: Emphasis on artificial lighting over natural sources
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Genre cinema using color for psychological and horror effects. Influence on later horror and thriller directors worldwide.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Influenced international horror cinema color aesthetics

defining_color_characteristics: Bold color symbolism using vast landscapes
Color as exploration of Chinese identity
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Post-Cultural Revolution exploration of Chinese history and culture through visual language. International art cinema recognition.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Established Chinese cinema on international stage, influenced global art cinema color use

movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Hong Kong's unique urban environment and cultural transition. Influence of Wong Kar-wai and Christopher Doyle collaboration.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Influenced international urban cinema aesthetics and neon-lit cinematography

defining_color_characteristics: Ability to manipulate specific hues within frame
technological_basis: Separation of capture and color timing
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Revolution in color control allowing unprecedented stylization. Democratization of color grading tools leading to aesthetic homogenization concerns.
key_films: O Brother, Where Art Thou? (first complete DI)
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Transformed film color aesthetics, enabled new visual styles but also led to color homogenization concerns

defining_color_characteristics: Acid greens, blacklight purples, sodium vapor oranges
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Contemporary urban alienation and digital age anxiety. Distinct from nostalgic 1980s neon aesthetics.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Defining contemporary genre cinema aesthetics, influencing music videos and advertising

defining_color_characteristics: Deliberate choice of black-and-white in color era
Different textures: grain vs. clean digital
technological_basis: Color film stock shot for black-and-white
movements_and_periods.ideological_context: Artistic choice emphasizing form over realism, nostalgia for classical cinema, or formal experimentation.
movements_and_periods.cultural_impact: Maintains black-and-white as viable artistic choice, creates dialogue with film history

extraction_notes.methodology: Movements extracted based on source document's historical periodization and color characteristics
extraction_notes.scope: Covers major international movements with documented color approaches
extraction_notes.technological_emphasis: Maintains connection between technology and aesthetic choices
extraction_notes.cultural_context: Preserves ideological and cultural contexts of each movement
